description: Add a tmux watchdog that automatically restores cron-system when the cron runtime dies.

userStories.title: Add cron runtime watchdog
userStories.description: As the harness operator, I want a supervisor for cron-system so scheduled control-plane jobs recover after the runtime tmux session dies.
acceptanceCriteria: .devcontainer/entrypoint.sh starts an idempotent cron-watchdog tmux session when scripts/cron-runtime.ts and tmux are available
cron-watchdog starts cron-system when absent and runs node --experimental-strip-types scripts/cron-runtime.ts from the harness root
cron-system logs still tee to /tmp/cron-system.log and watchdog logs tee to /tmp/cron-watchdog.log
The legacy system-cron migration guard prevents starting cron-system or cron-watchdog while system-cron exists

userStories.title: Guard the watchdog contract
userStories.description: As a maintainer, I want tests and eval coverage so future boot edits cannot silently remove cron supervision.
acceptanceCriteria: scripts/__tests__/entrypoint.test.ts verifies cron-watchdog, cron-system restart, log paths, and legacy system-cron guard
evals/probes/cron-watchdog.sh exits 0 against the implemented contract and reports REGRESSION on missing terms
pnpm test:scripts and bash evals/probes/cron-watchdog.sh pass
userStories.notes: Implemented with Vitest and a Tier-A probe.

userStories.title: Document cron supervision
userStories.description: As an operator, I want the environment inventory and cron docs to name the watchdog and the recovery path.
acceptanceCriteria: context/TOOLS.md lists cron-watchdog and describes cron-system as supervised
crons/README.md explains cron-watchdog inspection, log paths, migration guard, and the kill-session recovery path
CHANGELOG.md records the fix under Unreleased
